channel.title: Early-Game on Dyson Sphere Program Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Early-Game on Dyson Sphere Program Guide

item.title: DSP Power Transition Guide (v0.10) - From Wind Turbines to Antimatter Without Blackouts
item.description:
Short answer: Never skip power tiers. Wind turbines handle early game (build 20-30). Thermal generators power mid game with refined oil or coal (one refinery feeds 3 generators). Fusion plants carry late game (deuterium rods last 4+ hours per plant). Antimatter is endgame only - skip it until you have a stable Dyson Sphere producing critical photons.

Power is the single most common reason DSP factories stop. A blackout cascades - miners stop, belts stop, power generators starve, more things stop. Understanding each power tier and when to transition prevents hours of troubleshooting.

item.title: DSP Vein Utilization Guide (v0.10) - Optimal Miner Placement and Proliferation for Maximum Output
item.description:
Short answer: Each Mining Machine covers a 3x3 grid of ore tiles. Place them edge-to-edge over the densest part of the vein. One Mk.III proliferator sprayed on the ore output increases effective output by 25% at the cost of proliferator materials. The Vein Utilization research (up to level 10) doubles your effective ore per node.

Ore veins are finite. Every iron ore, copper ore, and coal vein on your starting planet will eventually deplete. Mining optimization determines whether you run out of resources in 20 hours or 200 hours. This guide covers every mining optimization technique.

item.title: DSP Automation Guide (v0.10) - Build Your First Automated Factory in 10 Minutes
item.description:
Dyson Sphere Program does not hold your hand. Your first hour can feel overwhelming. Dozens of buildings, multiple resources, and no clear starting point.

Here is the exact 5-step path to get your first automated factory running.

Your First Factory: 5-Step Setup

Step 1: Place Your First Miner

Find iron ore on your starting planet. Open the build menu, select the Mining Machine. Place it directly on an iron ore patch. Connect it to power with a Wind Turbine.

item.title: DSP Mining Outpost Guide (v0.10) - Set Up Off-World Bases That Run Forever
item.description:
Every DSP factory runs on raw materials — and raw materials live on planets that are rarely the planet where your main factory sits. Mining outposts are how you get coal from a gas giant's moon, titanium from an ice world, and fire ice from the dark reaches of the galaxy without leaving your main factory's logistics grid.

This guide covers how to build mining outposts, position mining machines correctly, and connect them to your logistics network so resources flow automatically.

item.title: DSP Smelting Array Blueprint Guide (v0.10) - 3 Smelters Feed a Mk.III Belt Perfectly
item.description:
Smelting is where every DSP run starts, and where most players first feel the pain of scaling up. One smelter looks fine. Ten smelters look decent. Fifty smelters feeding a sprawling factory reveal every bottleneck you buried under "it should work."

This guide covers smelting from the first furnace on a new planet through to full production columns — the correct ratios, the belt speeds that matter, and the layouts that scale cleanly without requiring you to redesign everything at every tier.

item.title: Can't Find Titanium in DSP? Here's How to Get It (And Bring It Home)
item.description:
Your starter planet has no titanium.

That's not a bug. It's not bad luck. It's literally how the game works. The devs designed it this way to force you to figure out interplanetary travel.

Here's the quickest way to get titanium and why you shouldn't panic.

TL;DR: Fly to the planet next door. Mine the shiny blue rocks. Fly back with a full inventory. Build your first ILS to automate it. Done.

Step 1: Find Your Titanium Planet

Open your star map (M key). Look at the other planets in your starting system. One of them will have titanium veins. In the default cluster, your starter planet orbits the sun with two more planets — the one closer to the sun usually has titanium. Sometimes both do.
